title: Crispy Buttery Norwegian Salmon Stir-fry
description: A quick, low-carb stir‑fry that combines buttery salmon with fresh vegetables, finished with a sprinkle of Parmigiano‑Reggiano for an extra touch of indulgence.
instructions: Heat a large non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Add olive oil and let it shimmer.
Season the salmon pieces with salt and pepper, then push them to one side of the pan and add butter to melt.
Sear the salmon for 2–3 minutes per side until the edges are golden and the flesh is just cooked through; remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the sliced onion, bell pepper, and mushrooms to the same pan and stir‑fry for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.
Stir in the snow peas, garlic, and ginger, cooking another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Return the salmon to the pan, pour in the soy sauce and rice vinegar, and toss everything together until evenly coated and heated through.
Give the dish a final sprinkle of grated Parmigiano‑Reggiano, adjust seasoning with a pinch of salt if needed, and serve immediately.
Enjoy a crispy, buttery finish that’s both energizing and diabetic‑friendly.
